articles.title: Visa Overstayers Caught: Delhi Police Crack Down on Bangladeshi Nationals after tip off
articles.description: Delhi police apprehended five Bangladeshi nationals for overstaying their visas. The arrests followed a tip about suspicious individuals speaking Bangla. The group had arrived in Delhi from Bangladesh and planned to travel to Greece for employment. They remained in Delhi beyond their visa validity, awaiting job offers. Efforts are underway to apprehend an associate who assisted them.

articles.title: 'Part of process': RSS downplays Cockroach Janata Party buzz, says Gen Z has tremendous faith in India
articles.description: RSS Prachar Pramukh Sunil Ambekar downplayed concerns about the satirical "Cockroach Janata Party" (CJP), stating that open debate and differing views are normal in a democratic society. He emphasized India's aware populace, transparent elections, free media, and active public discourse, asserting that institutions and political parties are capable of handling such discussions independently.

articles.title: AAP Sweeps Punjab Civic Polls, Stakes Claim To 2027 Momentum As Opposition Cries Foul
articles.description: AAP has registered a commanding victory in Punjab's civic body elections, winning nearly half of all wards contested and securing control of several key municipal corporations. The results have strengthened Bhagwant Mann's political position ahead of the 2027 Assembly elections, even as opposition parties allege misuse of state machinery during the polls. The outcome is widely being seen as the first major electoral indicator for Punjab's next political battle.

articles.title: 'Vande Mataram is not national anthem': CPI leader ignites fresh controversy in Kerala
articles.description: Explore the Vande Mataram controversy in the Kerala Assembly. Understand the debate over national song protocols and political reactions. Read the full analysis here.

articles.title: SC allows Vinesh Phogat to participate in Asiad trials today
articles.description: The Supreme Court permitted Vinesh Phogat to compete in the 2026 Asian Games selection trials, while criticizing the Delhi High Court's handling of the case. However, the court questioned the wrestler for missing a mandatory doping test, emphasizing the importance of adhering to rules despite her significant contributions to Indian sports.

articles.title: Punjab civic polls: Akali Dal’s electoral slide continues, but Sukhbir Badal sees a silver lining
articles.description: Party insiders argue that AAP performed dismally in the 2021 local body elections but went on to sweep the 2022 Assembly elections. SAD, they says, could stage a similar comeback in 2027

articles.title: 'India preparing for Operation Sindoor 2.0': Army chief Upendra Dwivedi
articles.description: India is preparing for 'Operation Sindoor 2.0' as Army chief Upendra Dwivedi stated the military posture in response to Pakistan's actions remains in place. The operation, a continuation of last year's retaliatory response to terrorism, involves enhancing synergy among all three services for future warfare. The focus is on cautious deployment and troop protection in border areas.

articles.title: India, Italy to launch maritime security format, anti-terror finance task force
articles.description: India and Italy are boosting ties. A new task force will fight terror financing. Maritime security cooperation is also planned to ensure safe sea routes. Both nations are exploring industrial collaboration, leveraging India's growth and Italy's manufacturing expertise. This follows recent high-level discussions strengthening cultural and defense links.

articles.title: AAP wave in Mohali Municipal Councils too as party sweeps 5 of 6 civic bodies
articles.description: With win in 16 wards, BJP largest party in Nayagaon

articles.title: Surendran, Padmaja, Sobha ...likely for BJP national posts; More Kerala leaders may get key roles
articles.description: A major organisational reshuffle is also expected within the Kerala BJP. State president Rajeev Chandrasekhar is understood to have discussed with the national leadership the replacement of certain state office-bearers and district presidents

articles.title: Rahul, Kharge in attendance, Congress strategy meeting for Punjab sees Channi, warring in blame game
articles.description: It is learnt that Channi raised the question of poor performance of Congress in Gidderbaha. He is reported to have asked how the party would prepare for the Assembly polls when the home constituency of the state president itself was swept by AAP.

articles.title: US invasion incoming? Top American general meets Cuban commanders amid rising tensions
articles.description: Top US military commander Gen. Francis Donovan met Cuban military leaders near Guantanamo Bay amid escalating pressure from President Donald Trump on Cuba and renewed regional tensions.

articles.title: Hegseth says era of US subsidising defence of wealthy nations is over
articles.description: "The era of the United States subsidizing the defence of wealthy nations is over. We need partners, not protectorates," US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said

articles.title: "Politics can be a sensitive matter": Jaxson Dart addresses viral Trump rally criticism, reveals why political differences won’t divide his teammates
articles.description: Jaxson Dart addressed media at OTAs on May 29, explaining his decision to introduce President Trump at a New York rally on Memorial Day weekend. Dart cited his family's military and government background as the foundation for his respect toward the presidency, while acknowledging the political sensitivities involved. Linebacker Abdul Carter, who had publicly questioned the appearance online, reiterated his opposition but maintained that his relationship with Dart and the team's shared goals remain intact. Both players indicated that honest conversations have taken place inside the locker room.

articles.title: ‘Europe Get Ready’: Russia Unleashes ‘NUCLEAR TONE’ After Romania Drone Crash | ‘You’ve Entered WAR’
articles.description: Russia’s former president Dmitry Medvedev has issued a chilling warning to Europe after a drone incident in Romania triggered outrage across NATO countries. Medvedev claimed EU nations are already “direct participants” in the war against Russia because of their military support for Ukraine. He warned Europeans that their “peaceful sleep is over” and said more incidents could happen as the conflict escalates. The remarks came after Western leaders blamed Moscow for the drone crash near Romania’s border, while Russia accused Europe of fueling attacks on Russian territory through weapons, intelligence, and drone support for Kyiv.

articles.title: Iran Unveils DEADLY New ‘Arash-e-Kamangir’ Weapon, Destroys US MQ-9 Aircraft Near Hormuz | Watch
articles.description: Iran has claimed that its newly deployed Arash-e-Kamangir air defence system shot down a US MQ-9 Reaper drone near the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran says the interception proves it still possesses effective military capabilities despite months of conflict. While the claim remains unverified, analysts believe Iran has been investing heavily in mobile, low-cost air defence systems.

articles.title: Woman cop suspended for ‘custodial torture’ in Odisha faced action earlier too
articles.description: A departmental probe has also been ordered against Jena’s conduct based on which further action will be initiated against her, officials said. Jena did not respond to calls and messages seeking a comment.

articles.title: Best of BS Opinion: Quad relevance, fuel pricing, and a Cockroach Party
articles.description: From the implications of warming US-China ties and the future of the Quad to fuel-pricing reforms, SpaceX governance and youth discontent, here are today's key opinion pieces

articles.title: In rare outreach, top US military commander meets Cuban generals amid Trump tensions
articles.description: The top U.S. general overseeing Latin America met with senior Cuban military officials near Guantanamo Bay, discussing operational security. This rare meeting occurs amid Cuban concerns of a potential U.S. attack, following a recent CIA director visit and escalating U.S. pressure on the island.

articles.title: US Generals Reach Cuba, Meet With Cuban Military Officials In Guantanamo Bay
articles.description: A top US military commander met senior Cuban military officials at Guantanamo Bay in a rare high-level engagement between Washington and Havana. The talks come amid rising tensions, increased US pressure on Cuba, and warnings from Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel about the consequences of military action.

articles.title: D K Shivakumar to take oath as Karnataka CM on May 31 or June 1
articles.description: Congress legislators meet on Saturday to elect D K Shivakumar as CLP leader, with his swearing-in expected on May 31 or June 1. Cabinet talks continue in Delhi as Siddaramaiah and party leaders discuss ministerial berths.

articles.title: RANA, GARC launch awareness campaign against fake Cambodia immigration notice
HC seeks U.P. govt reply on Noida workers’ protest arrest petition
articles.description: The Allahabad High Court has asked UP to respond to a petition challenging journalist Satyam Verma's arrest amid Noida labor protests.

articles.title: State, federal authorities spar amid protests at US immigration detention center
articles.description: State, federal authorities spar amid protests at US immigration detention center

articles.title: Exam scams, unemployment fuel massive protest in Prayagraj
articles.description: The protesters raised serious concerns over alleged irregularities in various central and state-level recruitment examinations

articles.title: New Metro campaign using police to get bus riders to pay their fares
articles.description: Metro wants bus riders to pay their fare and is putting Metro Transit police officers on the buses to make sure they do.

articles.title: D K Shivakumar hails Siddaramaiah’s ‘unwavering commitment to social justice
articles.description: Shivakumar hails Siddaramaiah’s ‘unwavering commitment to social justice

articles.title: UAE Launched Strikes On Iran After April Ceasefire Announcement: WSJ Report
articles.description: The strikes mark a significant departure from its policy and reflect a far more aggressive posture than that adopted by several other Gulf nations.

articles.title: Satish Jarkiholi positions himself as next Ahinda leader
articles.description: Former minister Satish Jarkiholi on Friday said he would bear the Ahinda flag following Siddaramaiah’s resignation as the chief minister.

articles.title: Most immigrants won’t need to leave to get green cards, US now says
articles.description: The Department of Homeland Security clarified that most green card applicants can remain in the U.S. during processing, easing concerns from an earlier announcement. This decision follows widespread confusion and backlash, with DHS stating officers will continue to decide on a case-by-case basis, a long-standing discretionary authority.

articles.title: Cong slams Bandikui mob lynching, demands impartial probe
articles.description: Congress condemns alleged Bandikui mob lynching of Dinesh Meena, seeks impartial probe, arrests, police accountability, and compensation; Gehlot cites law-and-order lapse.

articles.title: 70 Child labourers rescued from Surat
articles.description: 70 child labourers under 14 from Udaipur were rescued in Surat by the Anti-Human Trafficking Unit; employer booked, children counselled and linked to education schemes.

articles.title: 'PM Silent, As Usual': Oppn Tears Into Govt Over CBSE, NEET
articles.description: Congress MP Rahul Gandhi has criticised the Modi government over issues related to the CBSE's on-screen marking system and NEET examination problems.

articles.title: Pronounce bail orders in a day: Supreme Court to high courts
articles.description: The directions have been issued by the top court on an application complaining of delays in uploading a high court judgement. The application claimed that the Jharkhand High Court had pronounced the judgement in December 2025, but the ruling was yet to be uploaded on its website or released to the litigant's counsel.

articles.title: Jan Suraaj demands SIT probe into girl molestation case
articles.description: Jan Suraaj Party urges Bihar DGP to form SIT in Patna hotel molestation case, probe hotel management role, protect victim’s family, and audit hotel security statewide.

articles.title: Signature scandal deepens: CID probes legitimacy of TMC LoP selection letter
articles.description: The CID is investigating a signature discrepancy concerning Trinamool MLA Sobhondeb Chattopadhyay's appointment as Leader of Opposition. Several MLAs have claimed they did not sign the proposal or were absent, prompting the CID to question party members, including Chattopadhyay himself.

articles.title: Punjab polls: AAP's win rejects divisive politics, says CM Mann
articles.description: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann lauded AAP's strong performance in local body polls, saying the combined seats of BJP, Akali Dal, & Congress can't equal AAP's. He hailed the win as a rejection of divisive politics in favour of development.

articles.title: Heatwave conditions forecast in June, rain may bring relief
articles.description: Chennai braces for June heatwave with 2-3 heatwave days, hotter nights and 40-41°C highs; IMD says above-normal rain may bring brief relief.

articles.title: Punjab polls: Warring alleges foul play, claims 750 docs rejected
articles.description: Punjab Congress chief Amrinder Singh Raja Warring has alleged that 750 documents of party candidates were rejected in the civic polls. CM Bhagwant Mann hailed AAP's massive win, claiming it's a mandate for development over divisive politics.

articles.title: Minor rape-murder case moving in right direction: HC
articles.description: Madras HC says Coimbatore 10-year-old rape-murder probe is on right track; reserves order on SIT plea as state awaits DNA results, seeks video removal.

articles.title: Supreme Court Pulls Up NTA Over NEET 2026 Leak | Centre Says PM Modi Supervising Reforms
articles.description: The Supreme Court on Friday sharply questioned the Centre and the National Testing Agency over the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak controversy, raising concerns about accountability, institutional failures and the trauma caused to students and families. During the hearing, Solicitor General Tushar Mehta informed the bench that Prime Minister Narendra Modi is personally monitoring the re-examination and reform process to ensure there are no further lapses. The Centre told the Court that new security mechanisms have been introduced ahead of the rescheduled NEET-UG exam on June 21. The NTA informed the Court that sweeping structural reforms have been implemented, including mandatory CCTV checks, forensic analysis of surveillance footage, mock drills, emergency preparedness plans and strengthened coordination with police and intelligence agencies. The Court questioned how the leak occurred despite regular monitoring by the High Powered Committee and observed that accountability within the NTA remained unclear. Stressing the need for lasting institutional reforms, the bench directed the Ministry of Education to file a separate affidavit explaining how the NEET examination process would be institutionalised on a year-to-year basis to prevent future controversies. The matter will now be heard again in July.

articles.title: Why is Trump mocking Jill Biden over Joe Biden’s controversial debate performance? What he said in latest post
articles.description: Trump attacks Jill Biden following her concerns about Joe Biden's health during a debate.

articles.title: FDA accuses Dabur plant of creating ‘fraudulent’ records to conceal manufacturing operations
articles.description: A Form 483 is issued at the conclusion of an FDA inspection when investigators observe conditions that may violate US drug manufacturing regulations.

articles.title: 'No Need To Sing Vande Matram In...' Pinarayi Vijayan Hits out at Centre, BJP After Row In Assembly
articles.description: Kerala opposition leader Pinarayi Vijayan has criticised the Centre and BJP over the Vande Mataram controversy in the state assembly. He alleged full rendition of the national song is part of RSS agenda and defended partial performance. BJP accused the assembly of insulting the national song during Governor Arlekar’s address. CPM and BJP exchanged sharp political attacks over protocol, secularism and historical usage. The state government clarified it had no role, saying the programme was organised by Lok Bhavan authorities.

articles.title: Global Prices Rise On Iran War Shock As US Inflation Hits Three-Year High: When Will It Stabilise
articles.description: Rising energy costs linked to the Iran war are feeding through into higher prices for American households, while consumer spending power is weakening.

articles.title: AAP Sweeps Punjab Civic Polls, Wins 862 Wards, Congress Distant Second
articles.description: Chandigarh, May 29 (IANS) The ruling Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) in Punjab on Friday achieved an impressive victory in 102 civic bodies by winning 862 wards of the 1,765 that have been counted till now. A...

articles.title: ₹1,000 Crore for Damage Control: The Real Cost of India's Exam Crisis
articles.description: The NEET-UG 2026 paper leak controversy and the CBSE Class 12 evaluation backlash may together cost India hundreds of crores in refunds, re-evaluations, operational expenses, and damage-control measures. Shreya Chandra examines the hidden financial burden of examination failures, the opportunity cost for India’s education system, and why recurring exam crises are becoming a national policy challenge.

articles.title: US, Iran 'Very Close' To Deal: JD Vance; Tehran Says MoU Texts Aren't Accurate
articles.description: Vice President JD Vance says only a few language points remain unresolved, while Iranian sources insist media reports on the proposed agreement are inaccurate.

articles.title: CBI, NTA Face Tough Questions, Assure Parliamentary Panel 'Corrective Steps Are Being Taken'
articles.description: During the meeting, members of the panel questioned both the NTA and the CBI on the lack of coordination between the agencies

articles.title: 'PM Modi personally supervising so there is no lacunae': Centre tells Supreme Court on NEET paper leak
articles.description: NEET UG 2026: Centre tells Supreme Court that PM Modi is supervising the June 21 re-test process. SC says paper leaks are traumatic and demands accountability.
